name: CLI Interface
description: Click-based command interface using a custom SafeGroup subclass for centralized exception handling. Pure delegation layer — no business logic. All commands delegate to session/engine modules via typed Pydantic models. Shared round-limit options via decorator factory. API key validation as explicit helper. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 application error, 2 usage error (Click automatic), 130 keyboard interrupt.

types.name: ExitCode
types.description: Process exit codes used by CLI commands.

types.name: RoundLimits

fields.name: min_understand
fields.description: Minimum rounds in understand phase before auto-proceed is allowed.

fields.name: max_understand
fields.description: Hard maximum rounds in understand phase.

fields.name: min_challenge
fields.description: Minimum rounds in challenge phase before auto-proceed is allowed.

fields.name: max_challenge
fields.description: Hard maximum rounds in challenge phase.

types.description: Configurable round limits for understand and challenge phases. Resolved from CLI flags, then env vars, then defaults. Validated by Pydantic model in models.py; this is the shape the CLI constructs.

types.name: RoundLimitSource
types.description: Where a round limit value was resolved from, in priority order.

types.name: SessionListRow

fields.name: status
fields.description: Session status: incomplete, completed, or abandoned.

fields.name: current_phase
fields.description: Current phase name (understand, challenge, synthesis, done).

fields.name: posture_display
fields.description: Posture field masked as '***' for display.

types.description: One row in the session list display table.

types.name: ResumePromptResult
types.description: User's response when offered to resume an existing session.

types.name: GitignoreSuggestion

fields.name: should_suggest
fields.description: True if .constrain/ was just created and is not in .gitignore.

fields.name: gitignore_exists
fields.description: True if a .gitignore file exists in the current directory.

types.description: Information about whether to suggest adding .constrain/ to .gitignore.

types.name: DisplayableArtifacts
types.description: Content of the two output artifacts for display via `constrain show`.

types.name: ApiKeyCheckResult

fields.name: key
fields.description: The API key value if found.

types.description: Result of checking for the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable.

functions.name: ensure_api_key
functions.description: Check that ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set in the environment. If not, raise click.ClickException with prescribed message. Returns the key string on success. Called explicitly by commands that need API access (default, new, resume).

error_cases.name: api_key_not_set
error_cases.condition: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable is not set or is empty string

error_data.message: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set. Export it or add it to your shell profile.

postconditions: Return value is a non-empty string
Return value equals os.environ['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']

structured_side_effects.description: Pure environment read, no mutation

functions.name: resolve_round_limits
functions.description: Resolve round limit values from CLI flags, falling back to environment variables (CONSTRAIN_MIN_UNDERSTAND, CONSTRAIN_MAX_UNDERSTAND, CONSTRAIN_MIN_CHALLENGE, CONSTRAIN_MAX_CHALLENGE), then to defaults. Validates that min <= max for each phase pair. Returns a fully populated RoundLimits struct.

inputs.name: min_understand
inputs.description: CLI flag value for --min-understand, None if not provided.

inputs.name: max_understand
inputs.description: CLI flag value for --max-understand, None if not provided.

inputs.name: min_challenge
inputs.description: CLI flag value for --min-challenge, None if not provided.

inputs.name: max_challenge
inputs.description: CLI flag value for --max-challenge, None if not provided.

error_cases.name: min_exceeds_max_understand
error_cases.condition: Resolved min_understand > resolved max_understand

error_cases.name: min_exceeds_max_challenge
error_cases.condition: Resolved min_challenge > resolved max_challenge

error_cases.name: invalid_env_var_value
error_cases.condition: An environment variable is set but not a valid positive integer

error_data.message: Invalid value for {env_var}: expected a positive integer, got '{value}'.

postconditions: result.min_understand <= result.max_understand
result.min_challenge <= result.max_challenge
All four fields are populated with positive integers

structured_side_effects.description: Reads environment variables but does not mutate state

functions.name: check_gitignore_suggestion
functions.description: Check whether .constrain/ directory was newly created and whether .gitignore already covers it. If suggestion is warranted, display a message via click.echo advising the user to add '.constrain/' to .gitignore. Called after session creation.

inputs.name: constrain_dir_existed_before
inputs.description: Whether .constrain/ directory existed before the session was created.

postconditions: If should_suggest is true, a suggestion message has been echoed to stderr

structured_side_effects.description: Reads .gitignore to check if .constrain/ is already listed
Echoes suggestion message to stderr via click.echo if applicable

functions.name: cmd_default
functions.description: Default command invoked as `constrain` (group callback with invoke_without_command=True). Checks for incomplete sessions. If found, displays summary of most recent and offers to resume it, mentions `constrain list` for others. If user declines or no incomplete sessions, starts a new session. Accepts round limit options. Validates API key before any API interaction.

inputs.name: min_understand
inputs.description: CLI flag --min-understand or None.

inputs.name: max_understand
inputs.description: CLI flag --max-understand or None.

inputs.name: min_challenge
inputs.description: CLI flag --min-challenge or None.

inputs.name: max_challenge
inputs.description: CLI flag --max-challenge or None.

inputs.name: click_context
inputs.description: Click context object. If ctx.invoked_subcommand is not None, this callback is a no-op (subcommand handles execution).

error_cases.name: api_key_missing
error_cases.condition: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set and no subcommand was invoked

error_data.message: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set. Export it or add it to your shell profile.

error_cases.name: session_load_failure
error_cases.condition: Incomplete session found but its state file is corrupted or unreadable

error_cases.name: engine_error
error_cases.condition: Engine raises an exception during conversation loop

preconditions: Called as Click group callback; click_context is a valid click.Context
postconditions: If no subcommand invoked: either a session was resumed, a new session was started and run to completion or interruption, or user quit
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on application error, 130 on keyboard interrupt

structured_side_effects.description: Checks for existing incomplete sessions
Creates or updates session state file via session module
Writes output artifact on synthesis completion
Writes output artifact on synthesis completion

functions.name: cmd_new
functions.description: Subcommand `constrain new`. Always starts a new session regardless of existing incomplete sessions. Validates API key. Resolves round limits. Creates session via session.create(), runs engine.run(). Checks for .gitignore suggestion on first run.

inputs.name: min_understand
inputs.description: CLI flag --min-understand or None.

inputs.name: max_understand
inputs.description: CLI flag --max-understand or None.

inputs.name: min_challenge
inputs.description: CLI flag --min-challenge or None.

inputs.name: max_challenge
inputs.description: CLI flag --max-challenge or None.

error_cases.name: api_key_missing
error_cases.condition: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set

error_data.message: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set. Export it or add it to your shell profile.

error_cases.name: round_limit_invalid
error_cases.condition: Round limit resolution fails (min > max or bad env var)

error_cases.name: session_create_failure
error_cases.condition: Cannot create session directory or state file

error_cases.name: engine_error
error_cases.condition: Engine raises an exception during conversation loop

error_cases.condition: prompt.md or constraints.yaml exist and user declines overwrite confirmation

postconditions: A new session JSON file exists in .constrain/sessions/
If session completed: prompt.md and constraints.yaml written to cwd

structured_side_effects.description: Checks if .constrain/ directory existed before creation for gitignore suggestion
Creates new session state file

functions.name: cmd_resume
functions.description: Subcommand `constrain resume [SESSION_ID]`. Resumes the most recent incomplete session, or a specific session if SESSION_ID is provided. Displays a session summary (phase, round count, problem model snapshot) but does NOT replay history to the LLM. Validates API key.

inputs.name: session_id
inputs.description: Optional session ID to resume. If None, resumes the most recent incomplete session.

error_cases.name: api_key_missing
error_cases.condition: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set

error_data.message: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set. Export it or add it to your shell profile.

error_data.message: No incomplete sessions found. Use 'constrain new' to start one.

error_cases.name: session_not_found
error_cases.condition: Provided session_id does not match any existing session

error_cases.name: session_already_completed
error_cases.condition: Provided session_id refers to a completed session

Session summary displayed before conversation resumes
If session completed: prompt.md and constraints.yaml written to cwd

functions.name: cmd_show
functions.description: Subcommand `constrain show`. Displays the content of prompt.md and constraints.yaml from the most recent completed session to stdout. Does not require API key.

error_cases.condition: No completed sessions exist

error_data.message: No completed sessions found. Use 'constrain' to start one.

postconditions: prompt.md content displayed to stdout with header separator
constraints.yaml content displayed to stdout with header separator

Reads artifact content for display
Reads artifact content for display
Displays artifact contents via click.echo

functions.name: cmd_list
functions.description: Subcommand `constrain list`. Lists all sessions with status, creation date, current phase, and posture (masked as '***'). Formatted as an aligned table. Does not require API key.

error_cases.name: no_sessions
error_cases.condition: No sessions directory or no session files exist

error_data.message: No sessions found. Use 'constrain' to start one.

error_cases.name: session_dir_unreadable
error_cases.condition: .constrain/sessions/ directory exists but cannot be read

postconditions: All sessions listed in reverse chronological order (most recent first)
Posture field always displayed as '***' to avoid leaking prompt strategy
If no sessions exist, a helpful message is displayed instead of an empty table

structured_side_effects.description: Reads all session state files for listing
Displays formatted session table via click.echo

functions.name: format_session_table
functions.description: Format a list of session summaries into an aligned text table for terminal display. Pure formatting function with no I/O.

inputs.name: sessions
inputs.description: List of SessionListRow structs to format.

preconditions: sessions is a non-empty list of SessionListRow instances
postconditions: Return value contains one header row and one data row per session
Columns are: ID, STATUS, CREATED, PHASE, POSTURE
All posture_display values are '***'
Rows are whitespace-aligned

structured_side_effects.description: Pure formatting function

functions.name: format_session_summary
functions.description: Format a session summary for display when offering to resume. Shows session ID, current phase, round counts, and a brief problem model snapshot. Pure formatting function.

inputs.name: session_id
inputs.description: The session identifier.

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Current session status.

inputs.name: current_phase
inputs.description: Name of the current phase.

inputs.name: round_count
inputs.description: Number of conversation rounds completed so far.

inputs.name: problem_summary
inputs.description: Brief text summary of the problem model so far.

postconditions: Return value is a multi-line human-readable summary string
Contains session_id, current_phase, and round_count

structured_side_effects.description: Pure formatting function

functions.name: confirm_overwrite
functions.description: Check if prompt.md or constraints.yaml already exist in cwd. If either exists, prompt the user for confirmation to overwrite. Raises click.Abort if user declines.

inputs.name: cwd
inputs.description: Current working directory path as string.

error_cases.name: user_declined
error_cases.condition: User responds 'no' to overwrite confirmation prompt

preconditions: cwd is a valid directory path
postconditions: Returns True if no files exist or user confirmed overwrite
Raises click.Abort if user declined

structured_side_effects.description: Checks if file exists in cwd
Checks if file exists in cwd
Prompts user for confirmation via click.confirm

functions.name: create_cli
functions.description: Factory function that constructs and returns the fully configured Click group with all subcommands registered. The group uses the SafeGroup custom class for centralized exception handling. This is the entry point used by the package's console_scripts entrypoint.
postconditions: Return value is a click.Group instance with SafeGroup as its class
Group has subcommands: new, resume, show, list
Group callback is cmd_default with invoke_without_command=True
Round limit options are applied to both group callback and new subcommand

structured_side_effects.description: Pure construction, no I/O

functions.name: safe_group_invoke
functions.description: Override of click.Group.invoke() in the SafeGroup subclass. Wraps all command execution in centralized exception handling. Catches KeyboardInterrupt (exit 130 with friendly message), click.ClickException (re-raised for Click to handle, exit 1), click.Abort (exit 1 with 'Aborted.' message), and unexpected exceptions (exit 1 with generic 'An unexpected error occurred: {type}: {message}' — no stack trace).

inputs.name: click_context
inputs.description: Click context object passed by Click framework.

error_cases.name: keyboard_interrupt
error_cases.condition: User presses Ctrl+C during any command execution

error_cases.name: application_error
error_cases.condition: Any click.ClickException raised by command logic

error_cases.name: user_abort
error_cases.condition: User aborts a confirmation prompt

error_cases.name: unexpected_error
error_cases.condition: Any non-Click exception escapes command logic

preconditions: Called by Click framework during command dispatch
postconditions: On KeyboardInterrupt: sys.exit(130) after echoing newline and 'Interrupted.' to stderr
On click.ClickException: re-raised for Click's default handling (exit 1)
On click.Abort: sys.exit(1) after echoing 'Aborted.'
On unexpected Exception: sys.exit(1) after echoing generic error to stderr
Stack traces never shown in normal operation

structured_side_effects.description: Writes error messages to stderr via click.echo

invariants: CLI module contains zero business logic — all domain operations delegated to session, engine, and models modules
All exceptions are caught by SafeGroup.invoke() — stack traces never reach the user in normal operation
API key is validated before any network call — ensure_api_key() called in cmd_default, cmd_new, cmd_resume
Round limit options use identical Click option declarations on both cmd_default and cmd_new via shared decorator factory
Environment variable fallback order: CLI flag > env var > default, consistently for all four round limit values
Posture is always displayed as '***' in cmd_list output — never revealed to user
All file I/O uses pathlib.Path, never os.path or raw string operations
Exit codes are consistent: 0 success, 1 application error, 2 usage error (Click), 130 keyboard interrupt
Output file overwrites (prompt.md, constraints.yaml) always require explicit user confirmation
.gitignore suggestion is displayed at most once — on the first session creation when .constrain/ is newly created
Session summary on resume shows current state but does NOT replay conversation history to the LLM
